retrospective.harder_than_needed: stories.json's S2 scope.includes bullet list named only joinRequestSender.ts, joinRequestHandler.ts, and (verify-only) PendingRequestsSection.tsx. It did not mention that the one production call site of sendJoinRequest (JoinRequestCard.tsx's handleSendRequest) also needed a one-line update to actually pass the requester's nickname through -- without it, the whole feature would be dead code in production (correct in isolated unit tests, but no real join request would ever carry a nickname). Confirming this was in-scope for S2 specifically (rather than a gap to escalate) required cross-referencing S3/S4/S5's own declared scopes and S5's note that 'no production code changes' land beyond S1-S4, which together establish that no other story in the epic would perform this wiring.
